channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This video company is positioning itself for a world without TikTok
item.description: As TikTok pleads with the U.S. Supreme Court to let it continue operating in the United States, one video company is taking advantage of the uncertainty around TikTok’s sister app.
item.dc:creator: Scott Nover, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok's Future in the US: A High-Stakes Supreme Court Case
item.description: The fate of TikTok hangs in the balance as the Supreme Court weighs the implications of a law requiring the app to divest its Chinese ownership.
item.dc:creator: Issie Lapowsky, Fast Company

item.title: Happy New Year! We begin the new year with the top five procurement themes that will shape the federal acquisition marketplace in 2025 and beyond
item.description: 2025 will see continued focus on supply chain resiliency, often revolving around support for and investment in the domestic industrial base.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok's Future in Jeopardy: What You Need to Know - 2023
item.description: TikTok could vanish from U.S. app stores by Jan. 19 if the Supreme Court does not block a federal law forcing it to cut ties with its China-based parent company
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The biggest addiction treatment provider in the US says it was hit by data breach
item.description: BayMark Health Services notified law enforcement and affected people of the breach, offering free identity theft monitoring.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: I’m a big PC gamer – these were the 3 best gaming handhelds announced at CES 2025
item.description: We've waited a while to see the veil lifted on the future of handheld gaming, and CES 2025 has done just that.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The future of customer experience: AI-powered technical documentation
item.description: Discover how AI can instantly decode technology and automates the creation of interactive documentation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fed up with Copilot in Windows 11? Microsoft pushing a new keyboard shortcut for the AI isn’t likely to help, and it could mess with your desktop mojo
item.description: When you next update the Copilot app in Windows 11, you might see a pop-up nudging you to use a new shortcut.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Wondering why your AirPods Pro case has started chiming? It's not a cry for help: it's keeping your hearing healthy
item.description: It's not a fault: Apple's simply monitoring your hearing health by increasing the chime rate in your neighborhood.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tableau certification guide: How to boost your data analytics skills
item.description: Data visualization platform Tableau is one of the most widely used tools in the rapidly growing business intelligence (BI) space, and individuals with skills in Tableau are in high demand.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cohere goes ‘North’ with agentic AI
item.description: Large language model (LLM) provider Cohere has unveiled its new agentic AI offering — North — a low-code platform that will allow enterprises to build and deploy agents across different business functions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: More than 4% PC shipment growth predicted for 2025, but not for what you expect, says IDC
item.description: PC sales certainly weren’t going gangbusters in 2024: They only grew a paltry 1% over 2023. According to new figures from IDC, vendors shipped 262.7 million PCs in 2024. But things did pick up a bit in Q4 2024: Shipments grew 1.8% over the prior year, reaching 68.9 million.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sony PlayStation is adding smell—yes, you read that right—to its games
item.description: Sony has unveiled a new gaming system that could allow PlayStation players to sniff their way through games like The Last of Us. Unveiled at CES 2025, the Future Immersive Entertainment Concept (FIEC) features a huge, room-size setup designed to push the boundaries of immersive gaming.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Watch Duty overtakes ChatGPT in the App Store as California wildfires spread
item.description: As devastating fires continue to rage in the Southern California area, an app that tracks wildfires with live maps and alerts has become the No. 1 free app in Apple’s App Store.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Despite potential TikTok ban, Bay Area parents to host digital safety events at their high schools
item.description: The social media platform TikTok partnered with the National PTA to launch an online safety awareness campaign at high schools throughout the country, which includes three high schools in the Bay Area.
item.dc:creator: Nollyanne Delacruz, The Mercury News

item.title: Unlocking business growth: How CIOs can blend strategy and innovation to drive success
item.description: The role of the CIO has evolved. Today’s CIOs are expected to do more than manage IT infrastructure and operations. A savvy CIO must go beyond traditional expectations, driving innovation and aligning technology with business strategy to deliver measurable business value.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use the new Lock object in C# 13
item.description: The C# programming language has provided support for thread synchronization using the lock keyword since its earliest versions. By using a lock statement, you ensure that only one thread can execute the body of the statement at a time. Any other thread is blocked until the lock is released.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Working with minimal APIs in .NET
item.description: If you’re building cloud-native applications with microservices, they need to be small. You don’t want code that comes with enough dependencies and required libraries to turn something that should be able to scale in milliseconds into megabytes of code.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Google faces privacy lawsuit as judge highlights data governance concerns
item.description: A federal judge in San Francisco has ruled that a privacy lawsuit against Google, alleging the company improperly collected personal data from mobile devices, can proceed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 7+ speedy steps to free up space on your Mac
item.description: Computers get clogged with digital “stuff” over time, and while we all like to think we’re good at managing all that D-detritus, there’s somehow never quite enough time to clean things up.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nvidia: Latest news and insights
item.description: When it comes to innovation and the technology to drive it, few companies today rival Nvidia. The company has leveraged its GPU expertise to become a dominant player in the AI market. Nvidia’s GPU pedigree dates back decades to high-powered processors originally built for gaming systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Building generative AI applications is too hard, developers say
item.description: When everyone touts the benefits of generative artificial intelligence, telling the world how much easier it will make all of our lives, many developers who actually have to build those miracle apps are of a different opinion, according to a new survey sponsored by IBM.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 6 fast ways to free up space on your Windows PC
item.description: Want to free up space on your computer? With the right tools, you can quickly eliminate gigabytes of unnecessary files and get back to work — or whatever else you use your computer for. I’ve long argued you don’t need a “PC cleaner” app. You mostly just need the tools built right into Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: China protests US sanctions for its alleged role in hacking, complains of foreign hacker attacks
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — China has slammed a decision by the U.S. Treasury to sanction a Beijing-based cybersecurity company for its alleged role in multiple hacking incidents targeting critical U.S. infrastructure, while the Chinese cyber security agency complained Monday of attacks on Chinese networks.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Create custom visuals on your iPhone with Image Playground in iOS 18.2
item.description: Apple's Image Playground transforms text prompts into images or animations in seconds, brining a world of creativity to the palm of your hand.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
